_source: Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE) India — SME Cluster Energy Benchmarks
_references: BEE-GEF-UNIDO Project: Foundry clusters — Belgaum, Coimbatore, Indore (12 SME clusters)
BEE-SME Programme Annexure-1: Rajkot = Foundry, Batala/Jalandhar/Ludhiana = Foundry
UNIDO EnView Cloud Analytics for Foundries — Furnace SEC: 0.601 GCal/tonne, GTG SEC: 0.624 GCal/tonne
BEE Impact Report 2021-22: MSME Sector, GEF-UNIDO programme achievements
International Energy Agency / IPCC — Foundry energy benchmarks
_units: kWh per tonne of finished casting
_notes: UNIDO EnView dashboard for foundries shows Furnace SEC ~0.601 GCal/tonne = ~699 kWh/tonne and GTG SEC ~0.624 GCal/tonne = ~726 kWh/tonne for grey iron. Coimbatore foundry cluster: 500+ units, benchmarked under GEF-UNIDO. Belgaum cluster used biomass gasifier for sand drying as pilot.

grey_iron.notes: Sand casting with cupola or induction furnace. UNIDO EnView dashboard reports ~699 kWh/tonne for Coimbatore/Belgaum clusters. Yield 65-75% typical for sand casting including gating/risering losses.
grey_iron.source_datapoint: UNIDO EnView: Furnace SEC 0.601 GCal/tonne = 699 kWh/tonne (Belgaum foundry cluster)

ductile_iron.notes: Magnesium treatment adds process energy and heat loss. Lower yield than grey iron due to treatment losses.

grey_iron_cupola.notes: Traditional cupola furnace — less efficient than induction. Common in Rajkot, Coimbatore MSME foundries. Higher SEC due to coke combustion inefficiencies.

aluminium_die.notes: High pressure die casting. Good yield due to precision tooling. Lower SEC due to aluminium melting point ~660 deg C.

aluminium_sand.notes: Gravity sand casting. Lower yield than die casting due to gating system losses.

aluminium_gravity.notes: Gravity die (permanent mould) casting — intermediate between sand and HPDC.

brass.notes: Jamnagar brass cluster benchmark (BEE-GEF-UNIDO). Lower melting temperature ~900 deg C. High recycled content typical in Indian MSME context.

zinc_alloy.notes: Zinc die casting — very low melting point ~420 deg C. Most energy-efficient casting process.

emission_factors._source: IPCC AR5 / World Steel Association / International Aluminium Institute / IZA

grid_emission_factor._source: CEA CO2 Baseline Database for Indian Power Sector, Version 18 (2023-24)
